# UC-22.8.9 ¬∑ SOC 2 CC1 ‚Äî Board and Committee ICT Oversight Evidence Trail

## Description

CC1 expects governance bodies to exercise oversight of system risks. Indexing attestations, meeting minutes extracts, and risk-committee action items proves that board-level review occurred on schedule and that open items were tracked to closure.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes governance, itsm and sourcetypes grc:board_evidence, snow:task. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map `topic` values to your risk taxonomy; (2) require `evidence_id` on every quarterly pack upload; (3) alert when `status!=closed` and `days_open>90`; (4) restrict index to compliance/GRC roles; (5) export quarterly PDF evidence list from the same search for auditors. Indexes required: `index=governance`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:board_evidence`, `sourcetype=snow:task`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=governance` `sourcetype="grc:board_evidence"` (evidence_id, committee, topic, decision, evidence_date, owner, status); optional `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:task"` for remediation tasks linked by `evidence_id`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map `topic` values to your risk taxonomy; (2) require `evidence_id` on every quarterly pack upload; (3) alert when `status!=closed` and `days_open>90`; (4) restrict index to compliance/GRC roles; (5) export quarterly PDF evidence list from the same search for auditors.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=governance sourcetype="grc:board_evidence" topic IN ("ICT","cyber","resilience","third_party") earliest=-400d
| eval evidence_ts=strptime(evidence_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_open=if(status!="closed", round((now()-evidence_ts)/86400,0), null())
| where isnull(days_open) OR days_open>90
| stats values(committee) as committees, max(days_open) as max_days_open, latest(decision) as last_decision by evidence_id, topic, owner, status
| sort - max_days_open
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC1 ‚Äî Board and Committee ICT Oversight Evidence Trail** ‚Äî CC1 expects governance bodies to exercise oversight of system risks. Indexing attestations, meeting minutes extracts, and risk-committee action items proves that board-level review occurred on schedule and that open items were tracked to closure.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=governance` `sourcetype="grc:board_evidence"` (evidence_id, committee, topic, decision, evidence_date, owner, status); optional `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:task"` for remediation tasks linked by `evidence_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: governance; **sourcetype**: grc:board_evidence. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=governance, sourcetype="grc:board_evidence", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **evidence_ts** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **days_open** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(days_open) OR days_open>90` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by evidence_id, topic, owner, status** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open evidence), Timeline (evidence_ts by committee), Single value (open items count).

## SPL

```spl
index=governance sourcetype="grc:board_evidence" topic IN ("ICT","cyber","resilience","third_party") earliest=-400d
| eval evidence_ts=strptime(evidence_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_open=if(status!="closed", round((now()-evidence_ts)/86400,0), null())
| where isnull(days_open) OR days_open>90
| stats values(committee) as committees, max(days_open) as max_days_open, latest(decision) as last_decision by evidence_id, topic, owner, status
| sort - max_days_open
```

## Visualization

Table (open evidence), Timeline (evidence_ts by committee), Single value (open items count).
